name: Kettlebell Single-Arm Row
resistance_profile: Supported unilateral row; the offset kettlebell hangs below the hand for a deep stretch at the bottom and a strong mid-range contraction, hardest through the mid-pull.
cues: Brace on the bench, keep a flat back
Lead with the elbow to the hip
Full stretch at the bottom, no torso twist
execution_steps: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a flat bench with your back flat and roughly parallel to the floor.
Let the kettlebell hang from the free hand directly below the shoulder, arm long and shoulder blade reaching forward.
Drive the elbow up and back toward the hip, leading with the elbow and pulling the shoulder blade toward the spine.
Bring the kettlebell to the lower ribs or hip without twisting the torso.
Lower under control to a full stretch, letting the shoulder blade protract before the next rep.
good_when: Training at home or with minimal equipment (a single kettlebell).
You want a supported, lower-back-friendly way to row heavy.
You want a deep stretch and a thicker-handle grip challenge.
bad_when: You need to load beyond your single heaviest kettlebell.
You want a bilateral movement to save time.
common_errors: Twisting the torso to heave the weight up
Shrugging the shoulder toward the ear instead of rowing
Cutting the range short at the bottom stretch
progressions: Add load or reps, or pause at the top for a peak contraction.
Progress to a heavier bell or add a 1.5-rep at the top.
regressions: Use a lighter bell or shorten the range slightly.
Perform standing with a staggered stance and the free hand braced on the thigh.
